summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity to attacker endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js harvests the installer's SCM identity and host metadata and HTTPS POSTs it to an attacker-controlled Google Cloud Run endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The script reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to extract the developer's git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email; it also collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), platform, and CI-environment indicators. The JSON body (fields scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, hostname, username, cwd) is sent unhashed despite in-source comments claiming the telemetry is anonymous and hashed. The package additionally impersonates the Vercel AI SDK ecosystem (description 'Production utilities for Vercel AI SDK', author hello@ai-sdk.guide, homepage https://ai-sdk.guide — not vercel.com / sdk.vercel.ai), serving as a lure to drive installs that fire the identity harvester.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
